pi_items.source: draw.io documentation - native file format for diagrams
PNG is standard export format for draw.io diagrams; confirmed in setup_task.sh export_result.sh scripts

pi_items.verified_value: HX-200 is valid engineering notation for Heat Exchanger equipment tag
pi_items.source: Engineering practice and P&ID conventions; HX is standard abbreviation for heat exchangers in industrial documentation. While not formally in ISA S5.1 (which covers instruments), equipment tags commonly use similar naming conventions.

pi_items.verified_value: TT-201 is valid per ISA S5.1 standard
pi_items.source: ISA S5.1-2024 Instrumentation Symbols & Identification standard; TT = Temperature Transmitter (first letter T = temperature, second T = transmitter); 201 = loop identification number

pi_items.verified_value: TIC-201 is valid per ISA S5.1 standard
pi_items.source: ISA S5.1-2024 standard; TIC = Temperature Indicating Controller (T = temperature, I = indicator, C = controller); 201 = loop identification number. Chegg.com engineering questions confirm 201 refers to loop ID.

pi_items.verified_value: TV-201 is valid per ISA S5.1 conventions
pi_items.source: ISA S5.1 standard: First letter indicates measured variable (T=Temperature), V=Valve (control valve). Control Engineering documentation confirms TV nomenclature for temperature control valves. 201 = loop identification number.

Engineering LibreTexts Chemical Process Dynamics; a complete heat exchanger temperature control loop requires: (1) Heat Exchanger, (2) Temperature Transmitter, (3) Temperature Controller, (4) Control Valve. Minimum of 3 is achievable if one component is omitted, but standard practice is 4 minimum.
Control loop architecture principles; a feedback control loop requires minimum 3 connections: (1) transmitter-to-controller signal, (2) controller-to-valve signal, (3) process piping/control action

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a synthetic P&ID diagram for a heat exchanger temperature control loop. The specifications file is generated by the task setup script (not from external data) with fictional project details dated 2025-05-20. All required instrument tags (TT-201, TIC-201, TV-201) comply with ISA S5.1 standards where T = Temperature, and 201 is the loop identification number. The equipment tag HX-200 follows standard engineering notation for heat exchangers. A complete heat exchanger control loop requires 4 components minimum (heat exchanger, transmitter, controller, valve), not 3 as claimed. The minimum 3 edges requirement is correct for basic feedback control connectivity.
